Startup company
triples.object: A startup or start-up is a company or project typically undertaken by an entrepreneur to seek, develop, and validate a scalable business model . [ 1 ] [ 2 ] While entrepreneurship includes all new businesses including self-employment and businesses that do not intend to go public , startups are new

triples.subject: Venture capital
Product/market fit
triples.object: Product-market fit is the degree to which a product satisfies a strong market demand.

triples.subject: Business model
triples.object: A business model describes how a business organization creates, delivers, and captures value , [ 2 ] in economic, social, cultural or other contexts. The model describes the specific way in which the business conducts itself, spends, and earns money in a way that generates profit . The process of bu

triples.subject: Cash flow
triples.object: Cash flow , in general, refers to payments made into or out of a business, project, or financial product. [ 1 ] It can also refer more specifically to a real or virtual movement of money .

triples.subject: Burn rate (business)
triples.object: Burn rate is the rate at which a company consumes its cash. [ 1 ] It is typically expressed in monthly terms and used for startups .  E.g., "the company's burn rate is currently $65,000 per month."  In this sense, the word "burn" is a synonymous term for negative cash flow . It is also a measure of

triples.subject: Series A round
triples.object: A series A is the name typically given to a company's first significant round of venture capital financing . It can be followed by the word round, investment or financing. The name refers to the class of preferred stock sold to investors in exchange for their investment.  It is usually the first ser

triples.subject: Lean startup
triples.object: Lean startup is a methodology for developing businesses and products that aims to shorten product development cycles and rapidly discover if a proposed business model is viable; this is achieved by adopting a combination of business- hypothesis -driven experimentation, iterative product releases, an

triples.subject: Y Combinator
Silicon Valley
Angel investor
triples.object: An angel investor (also known as a business angel , informal investor , angel funder , private investor , or seed investor ) is an individual who provides capital to a business or businesses, including startups , usually in exchange for convertible debt or ownership equity . Angel investors often pr

triples.subject: Seed money
triples.object: Seed money is the earliest financial support a new project receives, often arriving at a point when an idea exists but nothing concrete has been built yet. This first backing helps a venture take its initial steps and gives people working on it enough stability to continue developing their work. [ 1

triples.subject: Business plan
triples.object: A business plan is a formal written document containing the goals of a business , the methods for attaining those goals, and the time-frame for the achievement of the goals. It also describes the nature of the business, background information on the organization , the organization's financial projec

triples.subject: Minimum viable product
triples.object: A minimum viable product ( MVP ) is a version of a product with just enough features to be usable by early customers who can then provide feedback for future product development . [ 1 ] [ 2 ]

triples.subject: Customer development
triples.object: Customer development is a formal methodology for building startups and new corporate ventures. It is one of the three parts that make up a lean startup ( business model design, customer development, agile engineering). [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Disruptive innovation
Unicorn (finance)
Initial public offering
Small Business Administration
Mergers and acquisitions
triples.object: In business, mergers and acquisitions ( M&A ) are transactions where the ownership of a company , business organization , or one of their operating units is transferred to or consolidated with another entity. They may happen through direct absorption, a merger, a tender offer or a hostile takeover.

triples.subject: Bankruptcy
Market research
triples.object: Market research is an organized effort to gather information about target markets and customers. It involves understanding who they are and what they need. [ 1 ] It is an important component of business strategy [ 2 ] and a major factor in maintaining competitiveness . Market research helps to ident

triples.subject: Competition (economics)
Scaling (computing)
List of unicorn startup companies
triples.object: This is a list of unicorn startup companies :

triples.subject: Entrepreneurs Roundtable Accelerator
triples.object: Entrepreneurs Roundtable Accelerator (aka ER Accelerator , or ERA ) is an American seed accelerator launched in January 2011. [ 2 ]

triples.subject: Startup studio
triples.object: A startup studio , also known as a startup factory , or a startup foundry, or a venture studio , is a studio-like company that aims at building several startup companies in succession. This style of business building is referred to as "parallel entrepreneurship ". [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Blue Startups
triples.object: Blue Startups is a venture accelerator based in Honolulu, HawaiÊ»i, United States. Founded in 2012 by entrepreneur Henk Rogers , his daughter Maya Rogers , and venture capitalist Chenoa Farnsworth, the accelerator supports early-stage technology companies with mentorship, investment, and access to g

triples.subject: Startup Poland
Stealth startup
triples.object: A stealth startup is a type of startup company that operates quietly and in silence to outsiders, avoiding public attention. This may be done to hide information from competitors (which may include non-disclosure agreements ), or as part of a marketing strategy to manage public image and generate ex

triples.subject: Finnish startup scene
triples.object: The startup scene in Finland has given birth to companies such as Angry Birds , Supercell , Wolt , and many others.

triples.subject: The Startup Wife
triples.object: The Startup Wife is a 2021 contemporary fiction novel written by the British-Bangladesh writer, Tahmima Anam , published by Scribner in the US and Canongate in the UK. The book is an introspection on the startup industry and its relation to technology, gender, and ambition. The book was long listed

triples.subject: 500 Global
triples.object: 500 Global (previously 500 Startups) is an early-stage venture fund and seed accelerator founded in 2010 by Dave McClure and Christine Tsai. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] The fund admitted a first "class" of twelve startups to its incubator office in Mountain View, California in February 2011. [ 3 ] They expanded to

triples.subject: Venture client
triples.object: A Venture Client is a company that purchases, uses and adopts startup solutions with the purpose of obtaining a strategic benefit. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Venture clienting enables companies to tap into new technologies, business models and ideas and foster a culture of agility and growth. [ 4 ]

triples.subject: Eric Ries
triples.object: Eric Ries (born September 22, 1978) is an American entrepreneur , blogger , and author of The Lean Startup , a book on the lean startup movement. He is also the author of The Startup Way , a book on modern entrepreneurial management.

triples.subject: Startup Weekend (organization)
triples.object: A Startup Weekend (also known as Startupweekend or SW ) is a 54-hour entrepreneurship educational competitive event, [ 1 ] in which groups of participants form teams around ideas the Friday evening, and work during the weekend to develop a working prototype, demo, and VC presentation by Sunday eveni

triples.subject: Attentive (company)
triples.object: Attentive (also known as Attentive Mobile ) is an American AI-powered mobile marketing platform that provides marketing automation designed to assist businesses with personalizing consumer engagement . [ 5 ]

triples.subject: Startup Ecosystem in India
triples.object: The startup ecosystem in India refers to the network of entrepreneurs, startups, investors, incubators, accelerators, academic institutions, corporations, and government bodies that collectively support the formation and growth of new ventures in India. triples.subject: List of venture capital firms
triples.object: Below is a list of notable venture capital firms :

triples.subject: Corporate venture capital
triples.object: Corporate venture capital (CVC) is the investment of corporate funds directly in external startup companies . [ 1 ] CVC is defined by the Business Dictionary as the "practice where a large firm takes an equity stake in a small but innovative or specialist firm, to which it may also provide managemen

triples.subject: Bain Capital Ventures
triples.object: Bain Capital Ventures LLC is the venture capital division within Bain Capital , which has approximately $160 billion of assets under management worldwide. The firm's early-stage investments have included Attentive , Bloomreach, Billtrust, [ 1 ] Docusign , Flywire, LinkedIn , Justworks, [ 2 ] Turbono

triples.subject: Venture capital in Israel
triples.object: Venture capital in Israel refers to the financial capital provided to early-stage, high-potential, high risk, growth startup companies based in Israel . The country's venture capital industry was born in the mid-1980s and has rapidly developed. Israel currently has more than 276 active venture capit

triples.subject: Social venture capital
triples.object: Social venture capital is a form of investment funding that is usually funded by a group of social venture capitalists [ 1 ] or an impact investor [ 2 ] to provide seed-funding investment, usually in a for-profit social enterprise , in return to achieve an outsized gain in financial return while del

triples.subject: Venture debt
triples.object: Venture debt or venture lending (related: "venture leasing") is a type of debt financing provided to venture-backed companies by specialized banks or non-bank lenders to fund working capital or capital expenses , such as purchasing equipment. Venture debt can complement venture capital and provide v

triples.subject: Norwest Venture Partners
triples.object: Norwest Venture Partners ( Norwest ) is an American venture and growth equity investment firm. triples.subject: Aster (venture capital company)
triples.object: Aster is a venture capital company specialised in the energy , manufacturing , and mobility sectors. Founded in 2010, it has operations in Paris , San Francisco , Beijing , and Tel Aviv . Aster manages more than â¬500 million in assets and investments in Europe , North America , and China .
